title: Jamalabad Fort
tagline: Jamalabad Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kudremukh range of hills, 8 km north of Beltangady town and 65 km from the city of Mangalore. It is also referred locally as "Jamalabad Fort" or "Gadaikallu".

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: St. Antony Church, Ujire
topPlaces.description: St. Antony Church is a Catholic church in Ujire, within the Belthangady Varado of the Diocese of Mangalore, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalmanja village
topPlaces.description: Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

topPlaces.name: Tulu Nadu
topPlaces.description: Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

topPlaces.name: South Canara
topPlaces.description: South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.

topPlaces.name: Ujire
topPlaces.description: Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: Charmadi Ghat
topPlaces.description: Charmadi Ghat (Charmady) is a ghatti beginning from Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada and ending in Mudigere taluk of Chikmagalur. It is one of the points in Western Ghats through which motorable road passes connecting Dakshina Kannada with Chikmagalur district.

topPlaces.name: Kakkinje
topPlaces.description: Kakkinje is a village located in Dakshina Kannada District of Karnataka State in India and comes under Belthangady Taluk. The village incorporates an eclectic mix of religious denominations, including Christians, Hindus and Muslims.

topPlaces.name: Dharmasthala Temple
topPlaces.description: Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. The deities of the temple are Hindu god Shiva, who is referred to as Mañjunatha, Hindu goddess Ammanavaru (meaning mother), the Tirthankara Chandraprabha and the protective gods of Jainism, Kalarahu, Kalarkayi, Kumarasvami and Kanyakumari.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — St. Antony Church, Ujire & around

items.note: St. Antony Church is a Catholic church in Ujire, within the Belthangady Varado of the Diocese of Mangalore, India.
Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.
Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

Charmadi Ghat (Charmady) is a ghatti beginning from Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada and ending in Mudigere taluk of Chikmagalur. It is one of the points in Western Ghats through which motorable road passes connecting Dakshina Kannada with Chikmagalur district.

Kakkinje is a village located in Dakshina Kannada District of Karnataka State in India and comes under Belthangady Taluk. The village incorporates an eclectic mix of religious denominations, including Christians, Hindus and Muslims.

Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. The deities of the temple are Hindu god Shiva, who is referred to as Mañjunatha, Hindu goddess Ammanavaru (meaning mother), the Tirthankara Chandraprabha and the protective gods of Jainism, Kalarahu, Kalarkayi, Kumarasvami and Kanyakumari.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jamalabad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jamalabad Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jamalabad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (52 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jamalabad Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jamalabad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jamalabad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Jamalabad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jamalabad Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are St. Antony Church, Ujire, Kalmanja village, Tulu Nadu, South Canara, Ujire.
